video.title: Racing Resumes For Final 25km | Tour De Suisse 2023 Highlights Men - Stage 7

transcript.clean_text: After discussions overnight, the organizers, teams, and family of the late Gino Mäder decided to continue with the Tour de Suisse. Stage seven would take the riders along the course that spanned nearly 180 km, starting in Tubach, going over the hills, and finishing up in Weinfelden. No general classification racing done. Times taken with 25 km to go. No bonus seconds, either. At the finish in Weinfelden after a long ride through the hills. The peloton, understandably, was still in mourning. And to that effect, three teams decided not to take the start. Bahrain Victorious, Intermarché, and Tudor Pro Cycling with a whole host of other riders. Those remaining offered their condolences, tribute, and thoughts to Gino Mäder, his friends, and family. After a minute's silence, the riders were underway. And there'd be no attacks, no breakaways. With times taken at 25 km to go, nobody who didn't want to race wouldn't have to race. Different riders from several teams took it in turns to pull everybody along and do the work. The race effectively neutralized until 25 km to go. It would be there that the general classification times would be taken. Only the final time trial now remains to decide the general classification. But that had been far from everybody's minds, as had the racing. But once they had an opportunity to honor the memory of the late Gino Mäder, the riders responded. Neilson Powless attacking, several riders going away on the final remaining categorized climb. Several riders from Ineos were there. Wout van Aert was there from Jumbo-Visma. And Remco Evenepoel was following the moves, too, in his rainbow jersey. Evenepoel attacked with 17 km to go. Ben Healy of Jayco AlUla could follow him just for a while, but Evenepoel was soon alone. And from then on, it would be a typical Evenepoel exhibition. Behind, they tried to get organized, but no chase was catching this man today. Almost a minute of a lead with 3 km to go, and Evenepoel had time to think about how he would honor Gino Mäder. And it was riding as fast as he could, honoring the sport, the rainbow jersey, and the memory of a special man who left this world too soon. A rainbow for Gino at the finish line at the end of stage seven in Switzerland. Dedicated to their fallen comrade. A difficult day for everybody on and off the bike. Behind, there was the sprint for the podium places, won by Wout van Aert. Third place on the day went to Bryan Coquard. Riders would roll in behind. Only the time trial left in the Tour de Suisse that this week has been marked by tragedy. Remco Evenepoel winning stage seven, Wout van Aert second, Coquard third with Mozzato, and Adam Yates fourth, and Bouhanni fifth. Mattias Skjelmose on the brink of winning his first ever stage race, but he will have to fight for that in the final time trial. 8 seconds between him and Felix Gall. Juan Ayuso is in third with 18 seconds of a delay. And at 46, there's Remco Evenepoel. Stage eight, time trial. This one will be raced. The Tour de Suisse is up for grabs, albeit in a rather subdued and difficult atmosphere. It starts in St. Gallen and ends in Abtwil, just kilometers away from where the late Gino Mäder was born and raised. A final 25-km test against the clock awaits in Switzerland, but everybody's thoughts are still with the family and friends of the late Gino Mäder.
